Alright, the no sales sale. So this is the game changer. This is your secret weapon to how you're going to be able to out compete and literally just get more for every single marketing dollar you spend and is one of the great equalizers for people who aren't as good at sales compared to those who are. So first things first, you're always going to still try to sell service. Right now that's your primary method of generating money and so you're still always going to try and sell that first. I want to make sure that I'm clear here. You do not want to sacrifice service sales in order to make product sales. Like my dude. I'm the one who gains from selling product. I'm telling you don't sacrifice service sales for product sales. Now that being said, if they still say no and you have lost them after using all the overcomes you can think of, right before the sale turns sour, what you want to do is use this. You know what? If this entire program required no money down what you want to do, it's still, they'll probably say yes. You're like, alright cool. So here's what I'm going to do because I still want to help you get results. You came in the door, I still want to provide goodwill, I still want to help you. I mean that. So what I'm going to do is I'm going to give you everything I just said. Meals, workouts, recipes. I'll still sit down with you, make you a custom plan and I'll let you start with everyone who's doing this. The only thing is you'll just do the workouts at home instead of doing them here with us. Does that make sense? Sound good? Okay. Now if they say yes, then you're like awesome. So I just want you to succeed and maybe after you do that you'll want to come on board in person or you'll send us a friend or you'll leave us a nice review. Cool? Can you do that? Alright. Great. So let's just do this. I'm going to give you an appointment deposit just for this Saturday to get started. I'll give it right back to you when you show up. We're not going to pair our lights with $50 deposits, I promise you. Alright? It's just because like I said, when we've done this in the past people just don't show. I did this without putting the deposit down and the likelihood that people show is super, super low. Right? And so this is just so that you don't stand me up because I'm going to be, instead of meeting with clients to train them, which is what I get paid for, I'm going to be sitting there waiting for you so that I can make this thing for you for nothing. Okay, so that's my only ask. Is that sound fair? Most times they're going to see us after you just drop the price from $600 to $50. Right? Now when they do show up, you're going to give them the deposit back very, very straightforward. Okay? You like sound fair? I'll give you, I'm going to give you everything for free and you'll let you start with everyone else. Cool? Great. Now, here's some logistics. Book the paid no-sale sales as one on twos. Okay? So two people per 30-minute time slot. That is key. So paid people one on two. If they still don't want to put a deposit down, then just say, you know what, fine. I get that you're worried about this. I'll still do this for you, but pinky-pron, please do not stand me up. I'm going to be taking time out of schedule to meet with you. Please, please show. All right, I'm going to send you reminders and you did show today. So I know you're a person of integrity, so you little integrity tie down. Yes, you're a person of your word. So when you say something you're going to do, you're going to do it. Right? Yes? Okay, that's the kind of person you are. I'm going to make this exception for you. I usually don't do that because that is the history, but you're not going to be different. You're better. Right? Yes. Okay, cool. So you really get, if they're not going to put the money down, then you really need to sell crap out of them for showing up. All right? But still put everyone who still won't put the money down into a bucket, just one single time slot that you have everyone come in. All right? Because you're still going to only have 40 or 50% show for those because they're unpaid. All right? So this way, that way you don't waste your time and have like a bunch of slots that don't show up. Now, special note, make sure that you do not book them the same time as people who bought the person in person program. So you don't want to have somebody who's paid a $50 deposit next to somebody who's paid $600 to be there. All right? Not a smart business move. All right? So you want to have people who are all doing, you know, who bought the service separate from people who are just paying the no-sale-sale deposit to show up and then obviously gets sold product. All right? Don't want to be clear. Don't do that. All right? So don't forget to send reminders to all clients. People who bought service, people who paid for the no-sale and people who didn't pay for the no-sale. Make sure you send reminders to everyone because it's just like you would for a normal sale. You still need to send reminders to people. Don't forget. Fun note for you, the average sale for product on no-sale is higher than people who bought service, which is crazy. So I mean, like, I think one of the gyms sent me their stats. I think they said they pooled $315 on average for each person who showed up for the no-sale and average ticket, whereas I think they sold like $2, $2.50 or $2.40 on average for people who were purchasing good bought service, which is like an unbelievable stat, which is super cool, I think, interesting from a psychology standpoint. But I think part of that is because, one, you have a ton of goodwill with these people because you're literally giving them all the stuff for nothing. And so it just makes sense. And typically those type of people are people who don't want to do the work as much and so they're a little bit more privy. They're more predisposed to just buying product. Okay? So congratulations, you now have the secret weapon that almost zero facilities are using to increase average ticket sales and allows you to make money from people who otherwise would say no, all right? And even create a second recurring revenue stream because you can continue to serve these people once a month and have them come in and check in with you, which is awesome. And then you can have this other service of people who are remote who don't train in your facility, which does not constrain your space. And they're basically like weight loss clients. They come in, they check in with you, they weigh in. And then like next month, you just set a time with them and you just keep them on the subscription because to be fair, the subscription that you're going to be getting paid on that is going to be between $80 and $170 on the subscription that they're on. So it's almost like having an EFT, but you have no fulfillment besides meeting with them for 15 minutes once a month. It's from dollars per hour standpoint, it's better margin than almost every service you have at the gym. So one of the advantages here is it allows you to outspend and outmarket everyone else and thereby help more people, all right? So that is the deal. That is the no sale sale. Use it wisely. It's very powerful and it's one of the great equalizers between people who are amazing salespeople and people who are not. The people who are not as good as salespeople have more at bats for the no sale so they can equalize, you know, can even out the equation a little bit.
